title: Thelypteris remotipinnata
intro: Thelypteris remotipinnata, also known as the 'Remote-pinnate Lady Fern', is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris remotipinnata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, a tincture, or a decoction to be used in traditional medicine, and it is also used in some herbal remedies for its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
taxonomy: Thelypteris remotipinnata is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is native to certain regions, particularly in Asia. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which comprises various species of ferns. The classification of Thelypteris remotipinnata is further categorized within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. Thelypteris remotipinnata is a part of the Thelypteris genus, which is closely related to the genus Cyclosorus.
